slides.title: What is a Random Forest?
slides.content: A random forest is an ensemble of decision trees used for classification and regression. It leverages bagging to reduce variance and improve prediction accuracy.

slides.title: How Random Forests Work
slides.content: Random forests create multiple decision trees during training and output the mode of the classes for classification or mean prediction for regression.

slides.title: Advantages of Random Forests
slides.content: Random forests provide higher accuracy, handle large datasets efficiently, and prevent overfitting compared to single decision trees.

slides.title: Key Hyperparameters
slides.content: The number of trees, depth of the trees, and number of features considered for splitting are crucial hyperparameters in a random forest.

slides.title: Applications of Random Forests
slides.content: Random forests are used in finance for risk assessment, in healthcare for disease prediction, and in marketing for customer segmentation.

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: Random forests are a versatile and powerful tool in machine learning, especially when dealing with complex datasets that require robust prediction models.
